title: One pattern, one year
summary: A young apprentice in Marrakech learns that a single tile pattern takes a full year to draw — and his teacher is not in a hurry.
body:
Yusuf is sixteen. He lives in Marrakech. He works in a small workshop near the old wall. The workshop makes zellige — small clay tiles in many colours.

The master is Mr Hamid. He is seventy. He has worked with tiles for fifty years. The workshop is hot. The floor is full of small pieces of tile.

On the first day, Yusuf wanted to cut tiles. He liked the sound of the hammer. He liked the colours — blue, green, yellow, white.

But Mr Hamid gave him a pencil. He gave him paper. He said, 'First, you draw. You draw one pattern. Only one. For one year.'

Yusuf was surprised. He said, 'One year? But I want to cut.'

Mr Hamid said, 'The hand cuts. But the head sees. If the head does not see, the hand cuts badly. You will draw the pattern every morning. After one year, your hand will know the pattern. Then you can hold a hammer.'

Now it is month four. Yusuf draws the same pattern every morning. It is a star with eight points. He draws it slowly. Some mornings, he is tired. Some mornings, his lines are not good.

Mr Hamid looks at his paper. He says, 'Better than yesterday. Not as good as tomorrow.' Then he goes back to his tiles.

Yusuf keeps drawing.

annotations.meaningVi: thầy / ông chủ nghề (người dạy nghề)
gạch men Ma-rốc (gạch nhỏ ghép thành hoa văn)
ngôi sao tám cánh (mô-típ truyền thống)
tốt hơn hôm qua (cấu trúc so sánh hơn)
quay trở lại (phrasal verb: go back)
tiếp tục vẽ (cấu trúc: keep + V-ing)

grammarNotes.title: keep + V-ing (continue doing something)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'keep + V-ing' nghĩa là 'tiếp tục làm gì đó'. Trong bài: 'Yusuf keeps drawing.' (Yusuf tiếp tục vẽ.) Đừng dùng 'to-V' ở đây. Sai: keep to draw. Đúng: keep drawing. Cũng có thể viết 'keep on + V-ing' với nghĩa nhấn mạnh hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'keep + V-ing' means 'continue doing something'. In the passage: 'Yusuf keeps drawing.' Do not use 'to-V' here. Wrong: keep to draw. Right: keep drawing. You can also say 'keep on + V-ing' for stronger emphasis.

translationVi:
Yusuf mười sáu tuổi. Cậu sống ở Marrakech. Cậu làm việc trong một xưởng nhỏ gần bức tường cổ. Xưởng làm zellige — những viên gạch đất sét nhỏ nhiều màu.

Ông thầy tên là Hamid. Ông bảy mươi tuổi. Ông làm gạch năm mươi năm rồi. Xưởng nóng. Sàn nhà đầy mảnh gạch nhỏ.

Ngày đầu, Yusuf muốn cắt gạch. Cậu thích tiếng búa. Cậu thích màu — xanh dương, xanh lá, vàng, trắng.

Nhưng ông Hamid đưa cậu cây bút chì. Ông đưa cậu giấy. Ông nói: 'Trước tiên, con vẽ. Con vẽ một hoa văn. Chỉ một thôi. Trong một năm.'

Yusuf ngạc nhiên. Cậu nói: 'Một năm sao? Nhưng con muốn cắt.'

Ông Hamid nói: 'Bàn tay thì cắt. Nhưng cái đầu thì nhìn. Nếu đầu không nhìn ra, tay sẽ cắt sai. Con sẽ vẽ hoa văn này mỗi sáng. Sau một năm, tay con sẽ biết hoa văn. Lúc đó con cầm búa được.'

Giờ là tháng thứ tư. Mỗi sáng Yusuf vẽ cùng một hoa văn. Đó là ngôi sao tám cánh. Cậu vẽ chậm. Có sáng cậu mệt. Có sáng nét vẽ không đẹp.

Ông Hamid nhìn tờ giấy của cậu. Ông nói: 'Tốt hơn hôm qua. Chưa tốt bằng ngày mai.' Rồi ông quay lại với gạch.

Yusuf vẫn vẽ tiếp.

options: In Marrakech, near the old wall
questions.explanation: 'He works in a small workshop near the old wall' in Marrakech.

options: Small clay tiles in many colours
questions.explanation: 'The workshop makes zellige — small clay tiles in many colours.'

questions.prompt: How long has Mr Hamid worked with tiles?
questions.explanation: 'He has worked with tiles for fifty years.'

questions.prompt: What did Yusuf want to do on the first day?
questions.explanation: 'On the first day, Yusuf wanted to cut tiles.'

questions.prompt: What did Mr Hamid give him instead?
questions.explanation: 'Mr Hamid gave him a pencil. He gave him paper.'

questions.prompt: For how long must Yusuf draw the same pattern?
questions.explanation: 'You draw one pattern. Only one. For one year.'

questions.prompt: Why does Mr Hamid want him to draw before cutting?
options: Because the head must see before the hand can cut well
Because pencils are cheaper than hammers
Because Yusuf is too young to lift a hammer
questions.explanation: 'The hand cuts. But the head sees. If the head does not see, the hand cuts badly.'

questions.prompt: What does Mr Hamid say about Yusuf's drawing?
options: 'Better than yesterday. Not as good as tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Direct quote from the second-to-last paragraph.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Real skill begins with patient looking, not quick action.
questions.explanation: The whole passage contrasts Yusuf's impatience with the master's slow method.
questions.hint: Think about the year of drawing.

questions.prompt: What kind of pattern does Yusuf draw?
questions.explanation: 'It is a star with eight points.'

blanks.explanation: The story is set in Marrakech.
Mr Hamid has worked for fifty years.
Mr Hamid gave him a pencil.
Draw the same pattern for one year.
The work in the passage is slow and patient.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Hamid says, 'Better than yesterday. Not as good as tomorrow.' Is there something in your own life — a language, a skill, a habit — where you could measure yourself by that sentence instead of by 'perfect' or 'finished'?
